heading: Provision of treatments
label: 300ff–26 Provision of treatments

children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: A State shall use a portion of the amounts provided under a grant awarded under to establish a program under to provide therapeutics to treat HIV/AIDS or prevent the serious deterioration of health arising from HIV/AIDS in eligible individuals, including measures for the prevention and treatment of opportunistic infections.section 300ff–21 of this titlesection 300ff–22(b)(3)(B) of this title
have a medical diagnosis of HIV/AIDS; and
be a low-income individual, as defined by the State.

ensure that the therapeutics included on the list of classes of core antiretroviral therapeutics established by the Secretary under subsection (e) are, at a minimum, the treatments provided by the State pursuant to this section;
provide assistance for the purchase of treatments determined to be eligible under paragraph (1), and the provision of such ancillary devices that are essential to administer such treatments;
provide outreach to individuals with HIV/AIDS, and as appropriate to the families of such individuals;
facilitate access to treatments for such individuals;
document the progress made in making therapeutics described in subsection (a) available to individuals eligible for assistance under this section; and
encourage, support, and enhance adherence to and compliance with treatment regimens, including related medical monitoring.

children.heading: Duties of Secretary
children.label: d Duties of Secretary
children.text: In carrying out this section, the Secretary shall review the current status of State drug reimbursement programs established under section 300ff–22(2) of this title and assess barriers to the expanded availability of the treatments described in subsection (a). The Secretary shall also examine the extent to which States coordinate with other grantees under this subchapter to reduce barriers to the expanded availability of the treatments described in subsection (a).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: List of classes of core antiretroviral therapeutics
children.label: e List of classes of core antiretroviral therapeutics
children.text: For purposes of subsection (c)(1), the Secretary shall develop and maintain a list of classes of core antiretroviral therapeutics, which list shall be based on the therapeutics included in the guidelines of the Secretary known as the Clinical Practice Guidelines for Use of HIV/AIDS Drugs, relating to drugs needed to manage symptoms associated with HIV. The preceding sentence does not affect the authority of the Secretary to modify such Guidelines.

children.heading: Use of health insurance and plans
children.label: f Use of health insurance and plans

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: In carrying out subsection (a), a State may expend a grant under to provide the therapeutics described in such subsection by paying on behalf of individuals with HIV/AIDS the costs of purchasing or maintaining health insurance or plans whose coverage includes a full range of such therapeutics and appropriate primary care services.section 300ff–21 of this title

children.heading: Limitation
children.label: 2 Limitation
children.text: The authority established in paragraph (1) applies only to the extent that, for the fiscal year involved, the costs of the health insurance or plans to be purchased or maintained under such paragraph do not exceed the costs of otherwise providing therapeutics described in subsection (a).

children.heading: Drug rebate program
children.label: g Drug rebate program
children.text: A State shall ensure that any drug rebates received on drugs purchased from funds provided pursuant to this section are applied to activities supported under this subpart, with priority given to activities described under this section.
